title: GRE Verbal Reasoning
description: Vocabulary in context, sentence logic, and reading comprehension.

questions.prompt: Although the critics found the novel ______, readers embraced its sprawling, unhurried storytelling.
questions.explanation: "Although" signals contrast: critics disliked it while readers embraced it, so the blank must be negative — "tedious."

questions.prompt: Select the word that most nearly means 'to make worse':
questions.explanation: Exacerbate means to make a problem worse. The others all mean to ease or improve.

questions.passage: Coral reefs occupy less than one percent of the ocean floor, yet they support roughly a quarter of all marine species. This disproportion has long puzzled ecologists, who note that the surrounding tropical waters are remarkably nutrient-poor. The prevailing explanation is that reefs recycle nutrients with extraordinary efficiency: symbiotic algae living within coral tissue capture and return nutrients that would otherwise be lost, allowing the ecosystem to sustain a density of life far beyond what its waters would seem to permit.

choices.text: describe how coral reefs were first discovered
explain an apparent paradox and the leading account of it
argue that reefs should be protected from pollution
compare tropical and temperate marine ecosystems

questions.explanation: The passage poses a puzzle (high biodiversity despite nutrient-poor waters) and then gives the prevailing explanation (efficient nutrient recycling). That is describing a paradox and its leading account.

questions.passage: Coral reefs occupy less than one percent of the ocean floor, yet they support roughly a quarter of all marine species. This disproportion has long puzzled ecologists, who note that the surrounding tropical waters are remarkably nutrient-poor. The prevailing explanation is that reefs recycle nutrients with extraordinary efficiency: symbiotic algae living within coral tissue capture and return nutrients that would otherwise be lost, allowing the ecosystem to sustain a density of life far beyond what its waters would seem to permit.
questions.prompt: According to the passage, the symbiotic algae contribute to reef life chiefly by:

choices.text: producing more offspring than other marine organisms
filtering pollutants out of tropical waters
retaining nutrients that would otherwise be lost
expanding the physical area covered by reefs

questions.explanation: The passage states the algae "capture and return nutrients that would otherwise be lost," which is the mechanism for sustaining the dense ecosystem.

questions.prompt: Her argument was so ______ that even her opponents conceded its logic.
questions.explanation: If opponents conceded its logic, the argument was clear and convincing — "cogent."
